FOL: (Manager(james) ∧ AppearsInCompany(james)) ⊕ (¬Manager(james) ∧ ¬AppearsInCompany(james))

If AppearInCompany(james) is true, then from premise 7, Manager(james) must also be true (since the second disjunct requires ¬AppearInCompany(james)).

From premise 3, James either has lunch in company or at home. If he has lunch at home, by premise 4, he works remotely. But if he's a manager, by premise 6, he doesn't work remotely. So if James is a manager, he cannot have lunch at home.
